v0.1.7:
‚Äî EN/RU localization of changelog entries: new `public/release-notes.en.json` and `descriptionEn` fields in `public/quick-patch-changelog.json`. The Updates panel picks the description language from the current UI language (`dl-lang`) and re-renders on `i18n:change`.
‚Äî Dates in the changelog are now formatted with the active locale (`ru-RU` / `en-US`).
‚Äî Updates panel layout: single ¬´Changelog¬ª section was split into two side-by-side cards ‚Äî ¬´Changelog¬ª (app releases) on the left and ¬´Quick-patch history¬ª on the right. Both stretch to the full width of the main area with matching 28px side padding and fill the remaining vertical space with their own scrollbars. The `Type` dropdown was removed; each card has its own independent All/Important filter.
‚Äî Quick-patch: fixed a stale `sha256` in `quick-patch/manifest.json` that caused ¬´Checksum mismatch¬ª on download (asset `overlay.css` was updated without syncing the hash in manifest). Added `scripts/qp-sync.mjs` + npm scripts `qp:sync` / `qp:bump` which recompute hashes using the same CRLF‚ÜíLF normalization as `src-tauri/src/quick_patch.rs::normalize_text_asset_bytes`, plus a CI job `.github/workflows/quick-patch-verify.yml` that fails any push/PR where `quick-patch/**` changes aren't hash-synced.
‚Äî New feature ¬´Find Config¬ª on the Dashboard: Tauri backend locates the Deadlock install via the Windows registry ‚Üí Steam ‚Üí `libraryfolders.vdf` (AppID `1422450`). A stat card shows whether `game/citadel/cfg/autoexec.cfg` exists; when the game is found but the file isn't, a ¬´Create autoexec.cfg¬ª button writes a default stub. New Rust module `src-tauri/src/game_config.rs` and commands `autoexec_status` / `autoexec_create`, exposed on startup via `ui_startup_snapshot`.
v0.1.6:
‚Äî EN/RU localization: new `src/i18n.ts` module with a UI dictionary (sidebar/nav, dashboard, profile bar, game status, Visuals/Profiles/Updates pages, modals and updater messages). Deadlock feature names (Auto Parry, ESP, Slide Enhancer, FOV, etc.) intentionally remain in English.
‚Äî Strings are swapped via `data-i18n` / `data-i18n-attr` attributes on markup, plus an `i18n:change` event for dynamic texts (updater status, game status). The selected language is stored in `localStorage` (key `dl-lang`).
‚Äî Custom `<select>` on the Updates page: the native `<select>` stays in the DOM as the source of truth for value and events, with a HUD-styled trigger + listbox rendered on top. Keyboard navigation (‚Üë/‚Üì/Enter/Esc) and a `MutationObserver` sync so external option re-renders (profiles) don't lose the selection.
‚Äî Native Windows titlebar now matches the app palette: DWM API immersive dark mode, caption color `#0a0a0a` (matching the window background), muted text, a faint greenish border `#0d130d`. Mica/Acrylic is disabled so the caption color is applied literally without tinting.
‚Äî Removed the light theme toggle (`dl-theme`) and its `index.html` init ‚Äî the app now uses a single dark theme.
‚Äî Large `src/styles.css` refactor (‚âà +1500 lines): unified cards, states, dropdowns, updated sidebar/header markup ‚Äî no visual regressions on existing screens.
‚Äî Dependencies: added `windows-sys 0.59` (features `Win32_Foundation`, `Win32_Graphics_Dwm`), windows target only.
v0.1.5:
Performance (before ‚Üí after):
‚Äî Main window startup: 3‚Äì4 separate `invoke` calls (version, game status, quick-patch CSS, occasionally a duplicate version) ‚Üí single `ui_startup_snapshot` ‚Äî fewer IPC round trips, faster first paint.
‚Äî Game process polling: fixed `setInterval` every 2.8 s even in the background ‚Üí adaptive timer: ~2.8 s when the tab is active, ~30 s when the window is minimized/hidden ‚Äî less CPU and `tasklist` load.
‚Äî Visuals tab: comparison images were validated and loaded at every startup ‚Üí deferred load on first open of Visuals ‚Äî faster cold start.
‚Äî Compare slider: DOM updates on every `input` ‚Üí coalesced through `requestAnimationFrame` ‚Äî fewer redundant repaints while dragging.
‚Äî Navigation: repeated `querySelectorAll` per click ‚Üí cached panel/menu node lists ‚Äî fewer DOM walks.
‚Äî Vite build: `build.target: es2022` ‚Äî less transpilation in the bundle from the same sources.
‚Äî Visuals Compare modal: HUD styling ‚Äî glass, gradient, backdrop blur, neon border and glow, a bottom OFF/ON bar with indicator, entrance animation (compare logic unchanged).
v0.1.4:
‚Äî Windows auto-update: NSIS and WebView2 in an explicit silent configuration ‚Äî `updater.windows.installMode: quiet`, WebView2 bootstrapper with `silent: true`, per-user install (`currentUser`), no installer language prompt.
‚Äî CI: release publish permissions (workflow read/write) + Tauri download cache for faster builds.
‚Äî Verification release after fixing GitHub Actions permissions.
v0.1.3:
‚Äî ¬´What's new¬ª and quick-patch changelog texts rewritten: bullet points describing the actual UI/logic changes, without generic build phrasing.
‚Äî GitHub release page description is now sourced from this file for the matching tag.
‚Äî Windows distribution is NSIS-only (`.exe`); MSI is disabled ‚Äî single installer, simpler updates, shorter release prep.
v0.1.2:
‚Äî Windows update mode: `installMode: quiet` ‚Äî the installer during auto-update no longer shows a window (NSIS runs in the background).
‚Äî Caveat: quiet mode does not request admin rights; if the app is installed machine-wide, the update may require a manual install or a per-user reinstall.
v0.1.1:
‚Äî Updates tab: the ¬´What's new¬ª list no longer widens the window when switching between ¬´releases¬ª / ¬´quick-patch¬ª ‚Äî `min-width: auto` removed from grid cells, text wraps (`overflow-wrap`).
‚Äî Right column with the changelog stretches to the full available width.
‚Äî Game process check: `tasklist` invoked without a popup console window (`CREATE_NO_WINDOW`).
‚Äî Bundle now ships only Rework version notes; old Electron-era entries were removed from the changelog.
v0.1.0: ‚Äî First public Rework on Tauri 2: main and splash windows, profiles (JSON in `%AppData%`), update checks and install, quick-patch channel from GitHub, settings and dashboard UI.
